title: DRINKS AND REFRESHMENTS
introduction: The quantities given below are calculated to be sufficient for fifteen glassfuls.

entries.title: BAVAROISE
ingredients: 1 pint of freshly made, boiling hot tea
One-third pint of the liqueur (Kirsch or Rum)
instructions: Work the powdered sugar with the egg-yolks in a saucepan, until the whole becomes white and reaches the ribbon stage. Then add consecutively: the capillary syrup, the freshly made, boiling hot tea, and the boiling milk; whisking briskly the while, that the drink may be very frothy. Complete at the last moment with the liqueur which is to characterise the Bavaroise, i.e., either Kirsch or Rum.

If the Bavaroise be flavoured with vanilla, orange or lemon, let the flavour infuse in the milk for fifteen minutes beforehand. If it be flavoured with chocolate, dissolve six oz. of the latter, and add the milk to it, flavoured with vanilla.
If it be coffee-flavoured, set three oz. of freshly-torrified and concassed coffee to infuse in the milk or flavour with one pint of freshly-made coffee.
Bavaroise is served in special glasses, and it must be frothy.

entries.title: BISCHOFF
ingredients: Syrup at 32° (sufficient to bring the preparation to 18°)
instructions: Put into a basin the Champagne, the "tilleul" infusion, the orange and lemon cut into thin slices, and enough syrup at 32° to bring the preparation to 18°. Let maceration proceed in the cool for an hour.
Strain the whole; freeze it like a Granité, and finish it with the liqueur-brandy.
Serve in bumpers.

entries.title: ICED COFFEE
ingredients: 1 quart of very cold, boiled milk
instructions: Pour the boiling water, gradually, over the freshly-ground coffee, and strain it gently. Put this coffee into a bowl with the loaf-sugar, and let the latter dissolve while the coffee cools.
Add the very cold, boiled milk, in which the vanilla should have infused, and the very fresh cream.
Freeze the whole in a freezer, taking care to keep the preparation almost liquid, and serve it in very cold cups.

entries.title: LEMONADE
ingredients: Zests of the rinds of 2 lemons
Thin roundels of lemon for serving
instructions: Dissolve the loaf-sugar in the filtered water. Add the juice and the zests of the rinds of the lemons, and let infusion proceed in the cool for three hours.
Pass the whole through a fine strainer; add the seltzer water, and serve with a thin roundel of lemon in each glass.

entries.title: PINEAPPLE WATER
ingredients: 1.5 lbs. of fresh or preserved pineapple
Seltzer water (sufficient to reduce the liquid to 9°)
instructions: Finely chop the pineapple; put it into a basin and pour over it the boiling syrup at 20°. Let it cool, and infuse for two hours.

Strain through a woollen bag; add the ice and sufficient seltzer water to reduce the liquid to 9°. Keep the preparation in the cool for a further twenty minutes, and complete it, when about to serve, with the Kirsch.

entries.title: CHERRY WATER
instructions: Stone the cherries, and rub them through a sieve. Put the purée into a basin with the stones, crushed in the mortar, and let the whole macerate for one hour.
Moisten with the filtered water, and strain the juice through a woollen bag, or muslin folded in two and stretched.
Add the ice and loaf-sugar, and put the whole in the cool for twenty minutes. Flavour, when about to serve, with the Kirsch.
The saccharometer should register 9° when inserted into this preparation.

entries.title: RASPBERRY-FLAVOURED, RED-CURRANT WATER
ingredients: 12 oz. of red and white currants
instructions: Rub the currants and raspberries through a sieve, over a basin. Add to the currant-water the filtered water, loaf-sugar, and the piece of washed ice.
Keep the whole in the cool for twenty minutes, and stir it from time to time with a silver spoon, that the sugar may dissolve.
Degree the same as in Recipe 2939.

entries.title: MELONADE
ingredients: A piece of very clean ice
Seltzer water (sufficient to reduce the syrup to 9°)
instructions: Rub the melon pulp through a sieve. Put it into a basin and pour over it the boiling syrup at 20°. Let the whole cool and infuse for two hours, and strain it through muslin or through a woollen bag.
Add the ice and sufficient seltzer water to reduce the syrup to 9°. Keep the preparation in the cool for a further twenty minutes, and finish it, when about to serve, with the orange-flower water.

entries.title: KALTSCHALE
ingredients: Half-lb. of peaches, peeled and sliced
4 oz. of ripe melon pulp, cut into dice
4 oz. of a mixture of raspberries and red and white currants, cleared of their stalks
Half a bottle of white wine
Half a pint of a mixed purée of strawberries and red-currants
instructions: Peel and slice the peaches and an equal quantity of pineapple; add the ripe melon pulp cut into dice, and the mixture of raspberries and currants. Put these fruits in a silver timbale and keep the latter on ice.
Set a little cinnamon to infuse in the boiling white wine; add the sugar and the zest of the lemon; and let the whole cool. Then add the mixed purée of strawberries and red-currants to this infusion.
Filter the whole, and complete it by the addition of the champagne.
Pour this preparation over the fruit, and serve the timbale very cold.

entries.title: ORANGEADE
ingredients: Juice and zests of orange rinds
Thin slices of orange for serving
instructions: Proceed as for lemonade, but use the juice and zests of orange rinds instead of those of lemons, and the juice of only half a lemon. (Recipe 2937)
Put very thin slices of orange in the glasses.

entries.title: PUNCH WITH KIRSCH
ingredients: A good half oz. of tea
instructions: Throw the tea into the boiling water, and let it infuse for ten minutes. Put the loaf-sugar into a punch or salad-bowl; strain the infusion of tea over the sugar, and dissolve the latter; stirring the while with a silver spoon.
Add the Kirsch, set it alight, and serve in glasses.

entries.title: PUNCH WITH RUM
ingredients: A good half oz. of tea
A few thin slices of lemon
instructions: Make an infusion with the tea and the boiling water. Strain it over the loaf-sugar, in a punch-bowl, and let the sugar dissolve.
Add the thin slices of lemon, and the rum, and set light to it. Serve with a slice of lemon in each glass.

entries.title: PUNCH MARQUISE
ingredients: Zest of the rind of 1 lemon
Half a pint of burnt brandy
instructions: Put the Sauterne wine, loaf-sugar, and the zest of the rind of the lemon bound round the clove into a small, copper saucepan. Dissolve the sugar; heat the wine until it becomes covered by thin white froth, and pour it into a punch-bowl after having withdrawn the zest and the clove.
Add the burnt brandy; set it alight and let it burn itself out.
Serve with a thin slice of lemon in each glass.

entries.title: ICED PUNCH
ingredients: Zest of the rind of 1 lemon
A good half oz. of tea
1 orange, peeled raw and cut into slices
1 lemon, peeled raw and cut into slices
instructions: Prepare a Marquise Punch as above; when the wine is hot, take it off the fire; throw in the tea, and let the whole infuse under cover for ten minutes. (Recipe 2946)
Pass the whole through a fine strainer; add the orange and lemon, peeled raw and cut into slices, and the heated rum. Set alight; leave to cool, and reduce to 15°.
Freeze like a Granité, and serve in glasses.

entries.title: HOT WINE
ingredients: Thin slices of lemon for serving
instructions: Pour the red wine over the loaf-sugar, set in a small, copper basin. Dissolve the sugar. Add the orange zest, cinnamon, mace, and clove.
Heat the wine until it is covered by thin froth, and then pass it through a fine strainer.
Serve with a thin slice of lemon in each glass.

entries.title: HOT WINE WITH ORANGE
ingredients: Half a pint of boiling water
instructions: Pour the boiling water over the loaf-sugar. Add the zest of the orange and let infusion proceed for fifteen minutes.
Withdraw the zest, and mix the heated Burgundy wine with the infusion.
Serve with a roundel of orange in each glass.

entries.title: WINE A LA FRANÇAISE
ingredients: 1 bottle of excellent Bordeaux wine or red Burgundy
Half of a lemon, cut into thin slices
instructions: Put the sugar into a salad-bowl, and sprinkle thereon a few tablespoonfuls of water, that it may dissolve. Add the Bordeaux wine or red Burgundy, and the half of a lemon cut into thin slices. Stir the whole well with a silver spoon and serve with a slice of lemon in each glass.
notes: Always remember to free the lemons and oranges used of all pips, which would lend a bitterness to the drink.

entries.title: CLARET CUP
ingredients: An equal quantity of orange slices
A few pieces of very clean ice
A few leaves of fresh mint
instructions: Put into a crystal bowl the loaf-sugar, the rind of the lemon and three slices of the latter, an equal quantity of orange, the strip of cucumber peel, the Angostura Bitter, and the Brandy, Maraschino, and white Curaçao.
Complete with the red wine and the Soda. Cover and let the whole infuse.
Strain, add the pieces of very clean ice and the leaves of fresh mint.
